question_prompt: Q12: How do you assess your own work ethic and workplace preferences?
panels: Q12: I am committed to working hard but oppose unpaid overtime and overwork, which I view as exploitation.
Q12: I am open to changing jobs frequently in pursuit of career advancement and better opportunities.
Q12: I am skeptical of hierarchical or authoritative management styles and prefer a more democratic workplace.
Q12: I believe older generations often misunderstand my approach to work and overvalue traditional work culture.
Q12: I believe that traditional 9-to-5 workdays are outdated and flexible hours should be standard.
Q12: I feel underappreciated when my work is measured by hours spent at work rather than the output or quality of my work.
Q12: I place a higher value on job satisfaction than on job stability, where willing to leave a stable job if it doesn’t fulfill me.
Q12: I prefer to work for startups or innovative companies rather than established corporations because they align more with my values.
Q12: I prioritize my mental health over making unnecessary work-related sacrifices.
Q12: I would consider working in a job that does not align well with my skills if it offered exceptional benefits or flexibility.
